history.content:
<thought>
I am facing East. The cell directly in front of me is open, and moving forward brings me closer to the goal area. Turning is unnecessary right now.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am still facing East, and the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. I need to change direction. Turning left will let me face North and avoid the lava ahead.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing North. The cell ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. To continue navigating around the obstacle, I should turn right to face East again.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Facing East again, the cell in front is lava, so I must not move forward. Since the lower path is blocked by lava, I should turn to find a safe route around it. Turning left will face North, which is the safer option from this position.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing North and the cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. To keep exploring for a path around the obstacle, I will turn right to face East.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Facing East, the cell directly ahead is lava, so I must avoid moving forward. I need to choose a safe turn to continue searching for a path around the hazards. Turning left will change my facing direction without stepping onto lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Facing North again, the cell ahead is still a wall, so I cannot advance. The open safe cell to my right was previously available from the earlier view, so I need to reorient toward it by turning right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Facing East, the cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. I should turn to look for a safe route around the obstacle instead of stepping into lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing North with a wall directly ahead, so moving forward is blocked. The only immediate safe option is to rotate and continue searching for a path, likely toward the open space on the side.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Facing East again, the cell ahead is lava, so I should not move forward. Since the goal is visible to the northeast but blocked by lava in front, I need to reorient to take the open safe cell first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing North with a wall immediately ahead, so moving forward is impossible. To make progress from this corridor, I should turn and use one of the open side cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Facing East, the cell directly ahead is lava, so I must not move forward. Turning is the safe choice to avoid stepping into lava while I search for a path around it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back facing North in the corridor. The cell ahead is open, so I can advance safely to continue exploring toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m still facing North in the same safe corridor. The cell ahead remains open, so the best move is to continue forward and keep exploring toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The view hasn’t changed, so I’m still at the same spot facing North. The forward cell is safe and open, so I should keep moving ahead.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
